title: Problem with Leliel
wikitext:
[]: Problem with Leliel. Post by flatulant » Mon Jan 01, 2007 6:05 pm Okay, so, how did Eva Shogouki get from the main body of Leliel to it's shadow? And how can a shadow be solid and bleed anyway? HELP!

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 01, 2007 6:12 pm Sorta like this Rei had been drawn by the shouting, had been about to interject something when they had both turned in synchrony and yelled at her to go away. But that had been enough to disrupt the momentum of the argument, and Asuka had stalked off, to stake out a good seat at Ritsuko's technical briefing seminar. â€œIt appears to exhibit an externally compactified dimension which is unrolled at the aperture â€” three nanometers into however much inside. It is probably an anomalous quantum state of the underlying spin foam â€” or rather a continuum of such states. The spherical body is possibly the â€˜other sideâ€™, the underneath of the disk which our underground investigations have found is not actually under the Angel. Within the unrolled dimension, we don't believe that there is any gravity â€” or at least any nearby source of any significance, given how slowly material subsided into it. We have no idea of how time progresses â€” whether there is any of the Minkowskian trade-off of time against space â€” in the â€˜interiorâ€™ region. â€œIt does appear to be hostile to normal matter.â€ And she had shown the retrieved end of the power umbilical, which seemed to have been eroded into some fractal mist. â€œHowever, we believe that the Eva's AT field will preserve its integrity. And assuming that Misaki initiated minimal hibernation mode, she will have maybe fifteen, sixteen hours of life-support. In her world-line.â€ All well and good â€” but what were they going to do about it? â€œThe perimeter of the disk, where one might have a cosmic string or similar space-time flaw, is shielded by an intense AT field. I propose that a sufficiently intense bombardment with N2 munitions, plus the two remaining Evas' ability to generate countering fields, be employed to attempt to rupture the perimeter, and unravel the whole geometry. It will take us most of the time we have left to arrange the strike. Questions?â€

[]: Post by flatulant » Mon Jan 01, 2007 6:18 pm One question, what's spin foam?

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 01, 2007 6:24 pm flatulant wrote: One question, what's spin foam?

[]: Post by flatulant » Mon Jan 01, 2007 6:30 pm Ow, my head hurts. Can you try and explain it in terms that a 9th grader with no prior knowledge of quantum mechanics and/or string theory can understand?

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 01, 2007 7:29 pm Why? Because it looks cool. Question answered.

[]: Post by master0rolando » Mon Jan 01, 2007 7:49 pm dude its TV...there are sometimes when...its just TV...they can do it cuz its TV

[]: Re: Problem with Leliel. Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Jan 01, 2007 8:00 pm flatulant wrote: Okay, so, how did Eva Shogouki get from the main body of Leliel to it's shadow?

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 01, 2007 8:04 pm I refer you to this thread , and ruthlessly lock this one.

But that had been enough to disrupt the momentum of the argument, and Asuka had stalked off, to stake out a good seat at Ritsuko's technical briefing seminar. â€œIt appears to exhibit an externally compactified dimension which is unrolled at the aperture â€” three nanometers into however much inside. It is probably an anomalous quantum state of the underlying spin foam â€” or rather a continuum of such states. The spherical body is possibly the â€˜other sideâ€™, the underneath of the disk which our underground investigations have found is not actually under the Angel. Within the unrolled dimension, we don't believe that there is any gravity â€” or at least any nearby source of any significance, given how slowly material subsided into it. We have no idea of how time progresses â€” whether there is any of the Minkowskian trade-off of time against space â€” in the â€˜interiorâ€™ region. â€œIt does appear to be hostile to normal matter.â€ And she had shown the retrieved end of the power umbilical, which seemed to have been eroded into some fractal mist. â€œHowever, we believe that the Eva's AT field will preserve its integrity. And assuming that Misaki initiated minimal hibernation mode, she will have maybe fifteen, sixteen hours of life-support.
